name: Hawaii Loa Ridge Trail
tagline: A steep Ko'olau ridge climb through native forest that delivers summit-caliber views in under 3 miles.
parking: Free parking inside the Hawaii Loa Ridge subdivision gate — the guard may ask where you're going, just say the trailhead
hours: Open sunrise to sunset daily; subdivision gate hours may vary
bestTime: Early morning for clear summit views and cooler temps on the exposed upper ridge
talkStory: About 5.5 miles round trip with 2,200 ft of elevation gain — it's a grunt. The trail climbs steeply through one of the last stands of native lowland forest on the Ko'olau Range, with koa, 'ohi'a, and native ferns. The upper ridge is narrow and exposed with views into Waimanalo and the windward coast. You'll need to pass through the Hawaii Loa Ridge residential area to reach the trailhead. The trail can be very muddy and slippery in the upper section. One of the best ridge hikes close to town for experienced hikers.
